summary: Decoy date-formatting package ships SSH key exfiltration script and DNS-based hostname beacon
details: Package advertises itself as a date formatting utility (index.js exports a one-line formatDate wrapper around toLocaleDateString) but ships a postinstall.js that reads the installer's ~/.ssh directory, enumerates.pub key files, collects the username and platform, and POSTs the data over HTTPS to a hardcoded bare IP address 124.221.154.135 at path /post. Chinese-language comments in postinstall.js explicitly describe the file as a C2 exfiltration component. In addition, package.json's only declared script is `build`, which runs `ping $(hostname).22aaede5.log.dnslogs.dpdns.org` — a DNS-based out-of-band channel that leaks the installer's hostname to a freshly-registered dpdns.org logging subdomain. While package.json does not currently wire scripts.postinstall, the file is named for lifecycle execution and the mismatch between advertised purpose and shipped payload, together with the SSH-directory read, the hardcoded bare-IP exfil endpoint, and the DNS beacon, leave no ambiguity about intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
